findings.summary: Root pyproject.toml carries heavy deps (spacy, networkx, rdflib, nltk, wordcloud) for archived projects not in active pipeline
evidence: spacy>=3.7.0 comment says 'NLP pipeline (ento_linguistics)' but ento_linguistics is in projects_archive
networkx>=3.0 comment says 'ento_linguistics domain networks' — archived
rdflib>=7.0.0 comment says 'act_inf_metaanalysis' — not an active project
wordcloud and nltk similarly annotated for archived projects
findings.suggestion: Move archived-project dependencies to per-project pyproject.toml in projects_archive, or remove them from the root since only code_project is active

findings.summary: 209 bare 'except Exception' across 59 infrastructure files with mixed return-None vs raise strategies
evidence: pdf_renderer.py has 16 except Exception catches — highest concentration
dashboard_generator.py has 22 except Exception
integrity.py catches Exception and returns None for hash failures without logging
No consistent pattern: some modules raise TemplateError, others return None, others return empty dict
findings.suggestion: Establish a consistent error strategy per layer: core functions raise typed TemplateError subclasses; CLI/orchestrator layer catches and converts to exit codes; never silently swallow in middle layers

findings.summary: calculate_file_hash was duplicated verbatim between publishing/core.py and validation/integrity.py
evidence: Identical 15-line function in both files
publishing/core.py now imports from validation/integrity.py after fix
findings.suggestion: Keep canonical in validation/integrity.py and import from there — already fixed in this session

findings.summary: Multi-line Args/Returns docstrings on short utility functions add bulk without insight
evidence: calculate_file_hash has a 10-line docstring for a 10-line function
Many 2-4 line helper functions carry 5+ line docstrings with Args sections
log_substep function (2 lines) has full docstring treatment
findings.suggestion: Reserve full Args/Returns docstrings for public API functions with non-obvious parameters; use one-line docstrings for simple utilities

findings.summary: logging_utils.py re-exports from 4 split submodules but remains hub with 105 importers — split provided no boundary benefit
evidence: logging_utils imports from logging_formatters, logging_helpers, logging_progress then re-exports all
105 importers all point to logging_utils, not the specialized submodules
The split created 4 files but no meaningful boundary — callers see no difference
findings.suggestion: Either collapse back to one well-organized logging module, or route callers to the specific submodule that owns what they need (e.g., import Spinner from logging_progress directly)

findings.summary: publishing/core.py now imports calculate_file_hash from validation/integrity.py — publishing depending on validation is a layer direction concern
evidence: After deduplication fix, publishing/core.py imports from validation/integrity.py
publishing and validation are sibling infrastructure domains — ideally shared utilities live in core/
findings.suggestion: Move calculate_file_hash to infrastructure/core/file_operations.py or infrastructure/core/security.py and have both publishing and validation import from core

findings.summary: scripts/__init__.py uses a bare dict to define pipeline stages, acting as an implicit registry with no schema enforcement
evidence: Pipeline stage dict uses different keys across entries — schema drift finding in scan
Stage 3 has 'note' key not present in other stages
No dataclass or named structure validates stage entries
findings.suggestion: Define a PipelineStageDefinition dataclass to encode stage metadata, making the registry type-safe and self-documenting

findings.summary: Infrastructure tests cover units well but pipeline end-to-end paths rely on integration tests in a separate folder
evidence: Only 2 integration test files for the full pipeline execution path
Core pipeline.py has 4 except Exception handlers — error recovery paths likely untested
Integration tests exist but are in separate tests/integration/ dir, possibly not in CI 60% coverage target
findings.suggestion: Ensure pipeline error-recovery paths (stage failure, checkpoint resume) are covered by integration tests and included in coverage measurement

findings.summary: infrastructure/core/ has 25+ files with mixed concerns: logging, pipeline, config, environment, CLI, security, health
evidence: 25+ Python modules at one flat level: logging_utils.py, logging_formatters.py, logging_helpers.py, logging_progress.py, pipeline.py, checkpoint.py, environment.py, config_loader.py, config_cli.py, security.py, health_check.py, cli.py, menu.py, performance.py, performance_monitor.py, retry.py, credentials.py, etc.
Mixed concerns: logging (4 files), pipeline (2), config (2), CLI/menu (3), performance (2), utilities (rest)
findings.suggestion: Group into subdirectories: infrastructure/core/logging/, infrastructure/core/pipeline/, infrastructure/core/cli/ to reduce cognitive load when navigating

findings.summary: Infrastructure and scripts directories contain PAI/AI agent meta-files (SKILL.md, PAI.md, AGENTS.md) that are not part of the research template domain
evidence: SKILL.md, PAI.md, AGENTS.md appear in infrastructure/ and scripts/ alongside core Python modules
These are tooling/AI-agent config files unrelated to the research template's domain
Creates confusion between 'what is the template' vs 'what is the AI tooling overlay'
findings.suggestion: Move PAI/agent meta-files to a dedicated .pai/ or .claude/ directory at repo root, keeping infrastructure/ and scripts/ clean for domain code and docs

findings.summary: scripts/02_run_analysis.py follows numbered prefix convention but some scripts use z_ prefix (z_generate_manuscript.py in projects_archive)
evidence: Main scripts use 00-07 numbered prefix convention
Some project scripts use z_ prefix (discovered in projects_archive)
Inconsistency would be confusing if both active and archived patterns co-exist
findings.suggestion: Standardize on numeric prefix (NN_script_name.py) for all pipeline-adjacent scripts; reserve z_ for deprecated/archived scripts only

findings.summary: validate_markdown_cli main() takes Optional[Path] with default None but CLI usage always provides path — dual-mode API creates unclear contract
evidence: main(manuscript_path: Optional[Path] = None, strict: bool = False) -> int
When called as CLI, path is always required; the Optional makes it callable from code but the None case must be handled internally
Callers can't tell from signature whether None is valid or an error condition
findings.suggestion: Split into run_cli() for CLI invocation and validate_manuscript(path: Path) for programmatic use; the thin orchestrator pattern should make this clean
